## Corpora Considered

Machine-readable catalog: [plot3_preregistered_source_catalog.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot3_preregistered_source_catalog.csv)

At the moment Plot 3 has `4` included source families and `23` explicitly considered but not included source families.

This table is the corpus-level admission audit: what was considered, why it looked eligible, what fields were actually confirmed, why it is in or out, and how many observations made it into the plotted layer.

::: {.dataset-table .source-audit-table .plot3-source-audit-table}

| Corpus considered | What it is / why possible candidate | Confirmed fields | Why in/out | # obs in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | Preregistered psychology-article key-question effects from Schäfer/Schwarz. Possible Plot 3 candidate because the public coded sample separates preregistered from non-preregistered articles and already provides N plus a converted r/d effect for each usable preregistered key effect. | Known: 93 preregistered coded rows, 89 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: analytic preregistration flag: yes (93); paper grouping: yes (93); paper D/N: yes (89); support status: not coded. | Included: 89 preregistered key-effect rows enter as support-not-coded preregistered results. The 4 remaining coded rows lack usable positive D/N. | 89 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Published psychology Registered Reports coded by Scheel et al. as preregistered hypotheses. Possible Plot 3 candidate because each row is an analytically preregistered confirmatory hypothesis and the local appendix table recovers D/N for the extractable subset. | Known: 71 preregistered hypothesis rows, 32 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: yes (71); support status: yes (32); journal provenance: yes (32); paper D/N: yes (32). | Included: 32 hypotheses enter. The remaining 39 Scheel rows are real preregistered hypotheses but do not have local D/N rows in the current table. | 32 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated papers [@tyner2026] | The SCORE/COS claim corpus was rechecked because its OSF manifest includes `orig_prereg-indicated.csv`. That file flags original papers with apparent preregistration, and the existing SCORE D/N parser already reconstructs paper-level D/N for a subset of those papers. | Known: 245 unique SCORE paper IDs marked `prereg=True`, 31 deduplicated paper-level D/N rows. Confirmed: paper-level preregistration indication: yes (245); paper grouping: yes; D/N: yes (31); claim-specific preregistration mapping: no; support status: not coded. | Included: 31 paper-level rows enter. The remaining 214 preregistration-indicated SCORE paper IDs do not currently have a deduplicated positive D/N row in the local SCORE candidate build. Caveat: the available flag is paper-level, so the table does not assert that every extracted claim was the exact preregistered primary hypothesis. | 31 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | PSA-CR001 was a preregistered 52-country COVID-19 framing project. Possible Plot 3 candidate because four primary confirmatory outcomes can be represented as pooled D/N rows rather than as nested country cells. | Known: 4 pooled primary outcomes. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: yes (4); support status: yes (4); pooled N: yes (4); pooled D: yes (4). | Included: the four primary PSA-CR001 confirmatory outcomes enter as pooled rows. Country-level cells stay collapsed here to avoid nested non-independent Plot 3 rows. | 4 |
| AACT x PubMed registered primary outcomes [@du2024] | ClinicalTrials.gov/AACT primary-outcome linkages were considered because trial registration can identify focal biomedical outcomes at scale. | Known: 2,720 registered primary-outcome rows in the earlier audit. Confirmed: trial registration: yes; analytic preregistration plan: no/insufficient; local Plot 3 D/N result rows: no. | Not included: registry metadata can support a comparator layer, but these rows are not yet analytic-preregistration result rows on the shared D/N axis. | 0 |
| Brodeur preregistered/PAP table tests [@brodeur2020] | The Brodeur economics corpus was rechecked because it is the other local source that produces thousands of preregistration-flagged D/N rows: many extracted table tests are from studies marked as preregistered or having a pre-analysis plan. | Known locally: 10,071 extracted economics test rows, 3,724 D/N-ready rows with preregistration/PAP/PAP-power flags, collapsing to 70 papers. Confirmed: preregistration flag rows: 3,547; pre-analysis-plan flag rows: 2,006; PAP-power flag rows: 1,720; D/N: yes; focal/main-result selector: no. | Not included: the preregistration/PAP flags are real and account for 3,724 D/N-ready extracted test rows, but all rows are coded as published table tests rather than source-selected main results. They are a good sensitivity lane candidate, not clean Plot 3 confirmatory-result rows. | 0 |
| COMPare prespecified outcome audit | COMPare looked like a missing Plot 3 source because the local parser recovers outcome-level rows and explicitly separates prespecified primary outcomes, prespecified secondary outcomes, and non-prespecified published outcomes. | Known locally: 72 trial reports and 1,171 outcome rows: 89 prespecified primary, 660 prespecified secondary, 422 non-prespecified publication outcomes. Confirmed: prespecified-outcome status: yes; trial/publication provenance: yes; effect estimate and N fields: no. | Not included: the prespecified-outcome labels are exactly the kind of audit trail we wanted to check, but the parsed rows stop at outcome text/reporting status. There is no D/N layer to plot. | 0 |
| CliniFact published trial primary-outcome rows [@zhang2025clinifact] | CliniFact was rechecked because these rows are journal-side clinical trial primary-outcome proxies with D/N on disk, so they are closer to published preregistered work than raw registry rows. | Known locally: 339 published trial primary-outcome rows, 339 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: journal provenance: yes; primary-outcome registry linkage: yes; D/N: yes; article-level analytic preregistration/hypothesis text: not confirmed. | Not included here: 339 D/N rows exist and are already represented in the published-paper endpoint surface. The current Plot 3 gate requires explicit analytic preregistered confirmatory rows, not only registry-linked primary outcomes. | 0 |
| ClinicalTrials.gov registry-result D/N comparator [@du2024] | ClinicalTrials.gov results were rechecked because they are the largest local preregistered-like bucket: trial records have registered outcomes and the parser recovers N plus p-value/effect-size proxies for thousands of primary registry-result rows. | Known locally: 7,722 registry-result rows, 7,722 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: registry outcome/result table: yes; N/effect proxy: yes; journal-published confirmatory result row: no; analytic plan/hypothesis row: no. | Not included: 7,722 D/N rows are real and local, but they are registry-outcome rows. They belong in a registry sensitivity lane unless Plot 3's inclusion rule is broadened beyond published analytic preregistration. | 0 |
| Communication privacy preregistered corpus [@masur2025privacy] | The communication privacy corpus was rechecked because it has an explicit preregistration PDF, local cleaned data, and many SEM/path outputs in the replication-pair file. | Known locally: 31 promoted/staged path rows and 8 downloaded source files. Confirmed: preregistration: yes; D/N or path-like rows: yes in Plot 1 context; standalone confirmatory result table for Plot 3: no; metric conversion policy: unresolved. | Not included: the preregistered corpus is real and local, but adding the path rows here would mix SEM/path metrics with the D/N confirmatory-result layer and double-count Plot 1-adjacent evidence. | 0 |
| ERN/Pe RRR OpenNeuro/OSF hubs [@ernPeRrrOpenNeuro] | ERN/Pe looked eligible because it is a Registered Replication Report with public OpenNeuro raw EEG and OSF analysis artifacts. | Known: OpenNeuro raw EEG plus OSF summary/analysis artifacts are local. Confirmed: preregistered replication status: yes; replication-side native metrics: yes; compact original-vs-replication D/N row table: no. | Not included: source is real and local, but it lacks a compact D/N table and the original-side result is missing from the local artifact set. | 0 |
| FORRT FReD / Replication Database | FReD was rechecked because it is a large replication database and the local clean export contributes many D/N rows to the replication and published-result layers. | Known locally: 843 FReD pair rows in the replication-pair file and 796 original published-target D/N rows in the published corpus. Confirmed: D/N: yes; original-replication pairing: yes; analytic preregistration flag for each source result: no. | Not included: FReD is already represented through Plot 1 pair evidence and Plot 2 original-target published evidence. The clean export does not make those rows standalone preregistered confirmatory results. | 0 |
| FReD archived workflow rescue queue | The FReD archive was rechecked because the freshness report found a possible rescue queue, including OSF Registries, ML1, RRR, Boyce, submissions, and OpenMKT-like sources. | Known locally: 343 converted/N-complete archive rows absent from the current clean export. A follow-up archive scan found 425 validated complete non-excluded rows overall, including 15 rows sourced from OSF Registries and 107 rows with a nonempty replication-preregistration field. Confirmed: N/effect conversion: partial; clean current-export status: no; duplicate/workflow filtering: unresolved; analytic preregistration: mixed/not confirmed. | Not included: the archive does contain preregistration-linked replication rows, including 15 OSF Registries rows, but they are replication-pair evidence rather than standalone Plot 3 result rows. Promoting them requires duplicate handling against existing FReD, Many Labs, RRR, and OSF-registries rows. | 0 |
| Linden focal/random psychology sample [@linden2024publicationBiasPsychology] | Linden was rechecked because the raw OSF materials include a preregistration artifact and the parsed focal/random effect rows are D/N-ready. | Known locally: 158 focal D/N rows and 158 random-effect D/N rows. Confirmed: D/N: yes (316); preregistration applies to the meta-research coding study, not to the sampled article findings. | Not included: the focal rows remain in the published-result layer and the random rows remain a comparator. The OSF preregistration does not make the sampled source articles analytically preregistered confirmatory results. | 0 |
| Many Labs 1-5 project-level replication rows | Many Labs 1-5 looked like Plot 3 candidates because the projects are coordinated large-sample replication efforts with preregistered or protocol-locked analyses and local D/N rows. | Known locally: 69 project-level D/N pair rows across Many Labs 1-5 in the replication-pair file. Confirmed: replication protocol status: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; independent single-result Plot 3 table: no. | Not included: local D/N rows exist, but they are already consumed by the replication-pair plot. Adding them here would double-count replication-side evidence unless Plot 3 gets a separate multi-lab-replication lane. | 0 |
| ManyBabies 3 registered-report materials [@manyBabies3Osf] | ManyBabies 3 looked eligible because it is a preregistered infant rule-learning multi-lab project. The repo has the OSF registered-report PDFs and public GitHub materials cached. | Known: target project expected roughly 15 lab/result rows in the harvest worklist. Confirmed: preregistered project materials: yes; final machine-readable site-level D/N result payload: no; promoted rows: 0. | Not included: preregistered materials are local, but the checked OSF archive and public GitHub repository did not expose final machine-readable infant result rows. | 0 |
| ManyClasses 2 classroom materials [@manyClasses2Osf] | ManyClasses 2 was rechecked because it is a coordinated classroom project with public OSF materials and a staged worklist estimate of multiple classroom result rows. | Known locally: 30 expected rows, 12 downloaded files. Confirmed: public OSF payload: yes; Terracotta/classroom data and scripts: yes; compact D/N confirmatory result table: no. | Not included: source files are local, but they require a separate education/classroom parser and metric policy before any Plot 3 points can be admitted. | 0 |
| Nordic trial registration-publication linkage | The Nordic trial-reporting database looked like the missing large clinical-trial preregistration source because it has thousands of trial-registration to publication links and records whether publications matched the registered design, population, intervention, comparator, and any outcome. | Known locally: 7,461 registry-publication search rows, 5,660 journal-publication rows. Confirmed: trial registration IDs: yes; publication DOI/PMID: yes where found; outcome/effect/N fields for D conversion: no. | Not included: the rows confirm publication linkage and matching metadata, but they do not contain exact result statistics or D/N fields for the preregistered point-estimate plot. | 0 |
| High-Replicability Research project [@protzko2024highrep] | High-Replicability Research was considered because it was designed as a preregistered counter-pattern source with many planned high-replicability findings. | Known: 48 project findings claimed in the audit trail; 3 local D/N pair rows recovered in the replication-harvest sidecar. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: yes; D/N: partial (3); non-retracted status: no. | Not included: the source fails the non-retraction gate, so even locally recovered rows stay out of Plot 3. | 0 |
| PSA replication-pair rows | PSA projects looked eligible because they are preregistered, multi-site confirmatory projects. The local repo has PSA-002, PSA-004, and PSA-006 D/N pair rows. | Known locally: 93 PSA D/N pair rows (PSA-002 object orientation, PSA-004 Turri, PSA-006 trolley) in the replication-pair file. Confirmed: preregistered/multi-site status: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; standalone non-pair Plot 3 table: no. | Not included: the PSA pair rows are already part of the replication-pair evidence. PSA-CR001 is the exception because it contributes four pooled preregistered confirmatory outcomes without an original-vs-replication arrow. | 0 |
| Registered Replication Reports Plot 1 pair rows | The live RRR rows looked like Plot 3 candidates because they come from preregistered replication reports and have local D/N encodings. | Known locally: 49 RRR/FReD-RRR D/N pair rows in the replication-pair file. Confirmed: preregistered replication status: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; independence from Plot 1: no. | Not included: the rows are real preregistered replication evidence with D/N, but their current role is original-vs-replication comparison. Plot 3 is reserved for standalone preregistered confirmatory result rows. | 0 |
| Registered Replication Reports per-lab rows | The RRR lab-level audit was considered because these are preregistered multi-lab replication analyses and the appendix documents recoverable per-lab D/N-style rows. | Known: 205 verified per-lab rows documented in the appendix across 10 RRRs. Confirmed: preregistered replication status: yes; current dedicated Plot 3 local source table: no; independence from Plot 1 pair rows: no. | Not included: real preregistered replication evidence, but adding lab rows here would double-count replication evidence already represented in Plot 1 until a separate within-project heterogeneity policy is written. | 0 |
| Retrieval-extinction rats preregistered replication [@luyten2017retrievalExtinction] | This source was rechecked because it is explicitly preregistered and the local harvest already promoted three original-vs-replication outcome rows. | Known locally: 3 D/N pair rows. Confirmed: preregistered replication: yes; D/N: yes; independent standalone Plot 3 row family: no. | Not included: the three rows are valid and already handled as original-vs-replication evidence. Plot 3 excludes them to avoid double-counting replication-pair arrows. | 0 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz non-preregistered comparator [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | The non-preregistered arm of Schäfer/Schwarz is useful as a within-source comparator because it has the same coded key-question-effect structure as the preregistered sample. | Known: 900 coded non-preregistered rows, 682 D/N-ready rows. Confirmed: analytic preregistration: no; paper grouping: yes (900); paper D/N: yes (682); support status: not coded. | Not included: 682 D/N-ready rows exist and are already eligible for the published-paper endpoint surface, but the rows fail the analytic-preregistration gate for Plot 3. | 0 |
| Self-control fMRI preregistered materials | The self-control fMRI replication looked eligible because the OSF node contains preregistration/scripts and the paper/OpenNeuro materials are local. | Known: four expected candidate rows in the harvest worklist. Confirmed: preregistration materials: yes; raw fMRI/paper mirror: yes; machine-readable D/N result table: no; native neuroimaging metrics: unresolved. | Not included: the source is documented and useful, but the current local payload does not provide Plot 3-ready confirmatory D/N rows. | 0 |
| Transparent Psi Project / Bem row [@kekecs2023tpp] | The Transparent Psi Project looked eligible because it is explicitly preregistered and the local combined raw data promoted one first-stopping-point D/N row. | Known locally: 1 promoted D/N pair row. Confirmed: preregistered replication: yes; original and replication D/N: yes; standalone confirmatory-result source independent of Plot 1: no. | Not included: this is a clean preregistered replication row, but its current analytic role is a shrinkage arrow against Bem's original result, not a standalone preregistered-result observation. | 0 |
| Twomey kinesiology article audit | Twomey looked eligible because its public article-level table includes a preregistration flag and sample size for 300 recent kinesiology articles. | Known: 300 article rows, 27 marked preregistered, 295 with sample size. Confirmed: article grouping: yes; preregistration flag: yes; exact effect/test statistic for D conversion: no. | Not included: this is a real public preregistration-aware article audit, but the released p-value fields are reporting-format/threshold metadata rather than exact numeric statistics for D/N reconstruction. | 0 |

:::

## Specific Observations Included

Machine-readable result-level file: [plot3_preregistered_results.csv](../data/derived/effect_inflation_dataset/plot3_preregistered_results.csv)

The specific-observation layer has `156` plotted preregistered result rows. It records source citation, result label, journal, `D`, `N`, and the source row number. Support calls are retained in the CSV as metadata but are not used for admission or visual encoding, because they mostly track the preregistered decision rule rather than a distinct effect-size concept.

::: {.dataset-table .specific-observation-table .preregistered-observation-table}

| Source | Specific paper/result | Journal | D/N |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Behavioral intentions (loss vs gain framing) | Affective Science | 0.10 / 15,772 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Policy support: individual autonomy subscale | Affective Science | 0.07 / 15,772 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Policy support: government power subscale | Affective Science | 0.08 / 15,772 |
| PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered rows [@dorison2022] | Information seeking (loss vs gain framing) | Affective Science | 0.02 / 15,772 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Remotely Close Associations: Openness To Experience And Semantic Memory Structure | European Journal of Personality | 1.29 / 516 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Delaying Access To A Problem-Skipping Option Increases Effortful Practice: Applicat | Computers & Education | 0.07 / 50,433 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - The Morality Of Action: The Asymmetry Between Judgments Of Praise And Blame In The | Journal of Experimental Social Psychology | 0.52 / 59 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - (Pre)Occupations: A Data-Driven Model Of Jobs And Its Consequences For Categorizati | Journal of Experimental Social Psychology | 0.54 / 183 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Ceos Imbue Organizations With Feelings, Increasing Punishment Satisfaction And Apol | Journal of Experimental Social Psychology | 0.29 / 302 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - How Internal Constraints Shape Interest Group Activities: Evidence From Access-Seek | American Political Science Review | 0.00 / 2,647,085 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Treating Depression With Physical Activity In Adolescents And Young Adults: A Syste | Psychological Medicine | 0.82 / 771 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Classified Or Coverup? The Effect Of Redactions On Conspiracy Theory Beliefs | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.10 / 2,524 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - The Effect Of Segregation On Intergroup Relations | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.20 / 1,081 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Preferences For Domestic Action Over International Transfers In Global Climate Poli | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.15 / 1,140 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Do Gender Quotas Really Reduce Bias? Evidence From A Policy Experiment In Southern | Journal of Experimental Political Science | 0.61 / 51 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2014 - Paying More When Paying For Others. | Journal of Personality and Social Psychology | 0.36 / 151 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - A Functional Analysis Of Two Transdiagnostic, Emotion-Focused Interventions On Nons | Journal of Consulting and Clinical Psychology | 0.27 / 855 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2015 - The Question–Behavior Effect: Genuine Effect Or Spurious Phenomenon? A Systematic R | Health Psychology | 0.09 / 37,452 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Imagine That! The Effect Of Counterstereotypic Imagined Intergroup Contact On Weigh | Health Psychology | 0.53 / 107 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Being Observed Magnifies Action. | Journal of Personality and Social Psychology | 0.46 / 100 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - Hypocritical Flip-Flop, Or Courageous Evolution? When Leaders Change Their Moral Mi | Journal of Personality and Social Psychology | 0.22 / 5,552 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Selection Bias, Vote Counting, And Money-Priming Effects: A Comment On Rohrer, Pash | Journal of Experimental Psychology: General | 3.13 / 12 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Atheist Horns And Religious Halos: Mental Representations Of Atheists And Theists. | Journal of Experimental Psychology: General | 0.40 / 273 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Experimental Evidence Of Discrimination In The Labour Market: Intersections Between | European Sociological Review | 0.24 / 400 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2016 - Worms At Work: Long-Run Impacts Of A Child Health Investment | The Quarterly Journal of Economics | 0.15 / 470 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2013 - Temperament Moderates Associations Between Exposure To Stress And Children's Extern | Child Development | 0.17 / 556 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Inferring Beliefs And Desires From Emotional Reactions To Anticipated And Observed | Child Development | 1.17 / 16 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - When Wanting To Be Fair Is Not Enough: The Effects Of Depletion And Self-Appraisal | Journal of Management | 1.36 / 83 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2015 - Leveling The Playing Field | Psychological Science | 0.40 / 167 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - A Large-Scale Test Of The Goldilocks Hypothesis | Psychological Science | 0.19 / 120,115 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Is Implicit Theory Of Mind A Real And Robust Phenomenon? Results From A Systematic | Psychological Science | 0.58 / 19 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - A Cognitive-Ecological Explanation Of Intergroup Biases | Psychological Science | 0.38 / 209 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Culture-Independent Prerequisites For Early Arithmetic | Psychological Science | 0.68 / 41 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2018 - Native Language Promotes Access To Visual Consciousness | Psychological Science | 0.82 / 28 |
| SCORE/COS preregistration-indicated original papers [@tyner2026] | 2017 - ‘Acting Wife’: Marriage Market Incentives And Labor Market Investments | American Economic Review | 0.58 / 60 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Kuppuraj 2018 — Online statistical learning reliability | Royal Society Open Science | 1.80 / 42 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Grubb 2018 — Former targets capture | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 1.10 / 80 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Zanolie 2014 — Magnitude×side Exp3 | Frontiers in Psychology | 1.09 / 20 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Voorspoels 2014 — Coalition encoding + cue | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.85 / 463 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | van ’t Veer 2015 — Liars liked less | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.60 / 155 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Dutilh 2017 — WM drift rate hard | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 0.58 / 916 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | van ’t Veer 2015 — Liars trusted less | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.56 / 155 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Dutilh 2017 — WM drift rate easy | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 0.49 / 916 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rotteveel 2015 — Lever congruency Exp2 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.49 / 50 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Steegen 2014 — Guess averaging immediate | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.40 / 471 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rowland 2014 — RIF Exp2 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.38 / 140 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Ong 2015 — Comfort food MTurk | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.38 / 366 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Verkoeijen 2014 — Spacing inductive learning | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.37 / 123 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Evers 2014 — Diagnosticity Study 1b | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.36 / 199 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rotteveel 2015 — Lever congruency Exp1 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.35 / 99 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Steegen 2014 — Guess averaging delayed | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.34 / 140 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Ong 2015 — Comfort food SG | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.32 / 198 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rowland 2014 — RIF Exp1 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.31 / 72 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | van ’t Veer 2015 — Skin temp observing liar | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.31 / 155 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Kvetnaya 2018 — Irrelevant speech spatial | J European Psych Students | 0.29 / 40 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Bombari 2017 — Body posture felt power | Comprehensive Results in Social Psychology | 0.28 / 200 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Grubb 2018 — Feedback capture diff | Attention Perception Psychophysics | 0.20 / 80 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Voorspoels 2014 — Race encoding + coalition cue | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.18 / 463 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Zanolie 2014 — Magnitude×side Exp2 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.17 / 24 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Jackson 2017 — Posture self-concept size | Comprehensive Results in Social Psychology | 0.15 / 128 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Bombari 2017 — Body posture gambling | Comprehensive Results in Social Psychology | 0.08 / 200 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Zanolie 2014 — Magnitude×side Exp1 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.08 / 20 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Evers 2014 — Diagnosticity Study 1a | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.06 / 253 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Rowland 2014 — Cue-independent RIF Exp3 | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.05 / 67 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Johnson 2014 — Cleanliness moral judgment Exp1 | Social Psychology | 0.01 / 208 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Johnson 2014 — Cleanliness moral judgment Exp2 | Social Psychology | 0.01 / 126 |
| Registered Reports preregistered-hypotheses corpus [@scheel2021] | Ong 2015 — Comfort food NL | Frontiers in Psychology | 0.01 / 176 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_0) | not coded | 0.41 / 250 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_1) | not coded | 0.08 / 197 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_10) | not coded | 0.41 / 811 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_11) | not coded | 0.17 / 216 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_12) | not coded | 0.49 / 570 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_13) | not coded | 0.05 / 736 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_14) | not coded | 0.48 / 176 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_15) | not coded | 0.20 / 220 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_16) | not coded | 0.22 / 541 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_18) | not coded | 0.91 / 68 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_19) | not coded | 1.29 / 38 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_2) | not coded | 0.06 / 650 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_20) | not coded | 1.64 / 195 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_21) | not coded | 2.73 / 381 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_22) | not coded | 0.25 / 71 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_23) | not coded | 0.37 / 122 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_24) | not coded | 1.54 / 363 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_25) | not coded | 0.26 / 732 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_26) | not coded | 0.09 / 400 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_27) | not coded | 0.12 / 794 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_28) | not coded | 0.08 / 95 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_29) | not coded | 0.77 / 127 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_3) | not coded | 0.93 / 627 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_30) | not coded | 0.48 / 202 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_31) | not coded | 0.19 / 468 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_32) | not coded | 0.67 / 50 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_33) | not coded | 0.06 / 302 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_34) | not coded | 0.04 / 2,141 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_35) | not coded | 0.02 / 2,284 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_36) | not coded | 0.06 / 377 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_38) | not coded | 0.18 / 1,250 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_39) | not coded | 0.02 / 98 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_4) | not coded | 0.75 / 45 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_40) | not coded | 0.10 / 360 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_41) | not coded | 0.06 / 223 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_42) | not coded | 0.93 / 58 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_43) | not coded | 0.65 / 300 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_44) | not coded | 0.13 / 139 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_45) | not coded | 0.43 / 440 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_46) | not coded | 0.44 / 297 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_47) | not coded | 0.20 / 35 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_48) | not coded | 0.34 / 92 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_49) | not coded | 0.20 / 356 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_5) | not coded | 0.20 / 4,754 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_50) | not coded | 0.33 / 266 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_51) | not coded | 0.12 / 240 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_52) | not coded | 0.29 / 356 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_53) | not coded | 0.94 / 148 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_54) | not coded | 0.22 / 2,053 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_55) | not coded | 0.13 / 1,618 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_56) | not coded | 0.20 / 264 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_57) | not coded | 0.47 / 81 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_58) | not coded | 0.35 / 374 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_6) | not coded | 0.08 / 55 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_60) | not coded | 0.41 / 272 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_61) | not coded | 0.28 / 205 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_62) | not coded | 0.27 / 268 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_63) | not coded | 1.03 / 545 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_64) | not coded | 0.34 / 87 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_65) | not coded | 0.36 / 252 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_66) | not coded | 0.70 / 177 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_67) | not coded | 0.80 / 101 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_68) | not coded | 0.80 / 379 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_69) | not coded | 0.59 / 297 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_7) | not coded | 0.41 / 905 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_70) | not coded | 0.14 / 1,673 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_72) | not coded | 0.84 / 51 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_73) | not coded | 1.34 / 37 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_74) | not coded | 0.23 / 80 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_75) | not coded | 0.29 / 400 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_76) | not coded | 0.18 / 120,115 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_77) | not coded | 0.49 / 621 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_78) | not coded | 1.34 / 37 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2018 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_79) | not coded | 0.54 / 332 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_8) | not coded | 0.16 / 680 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_80) | not coded | 0.03 / 386 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_81) | not coded | 0.24 / 53 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_82) | not coded | 2.63 / 1,462 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_83) | not coded | 0.09 / 242 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_84) | not coded | 0.22 / 199 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_85) | not coded | 0.47 / 471 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2014 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_86) | not coded | 0.18 / 463 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_87) | not coded | 1.50 / 90 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_88) | not coded | 0.63 / 36 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_89) | not coded | 0.19 / 631 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2017 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_9) | not coded | 0.61 / 257 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_90) | not coded | 1.37 / 20 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2016 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_91) | not coded | 0.03 / 1,894 |
| Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered psychology articles [@schaefer2019meaningfulness] | 2015 preregistered key-question effect (schaefer_2019_preg_92) | not coded | 1.15 / 79 |

:::

### What Is Still Missing

- The included core now combines Registered Reports, PSA-CR001 pooled preregistered outcomes, and the Schäfer/Schwarz preregistered key-effect sample.
- The expanded considered-but-not-included list now names the major local preregistered-like sidecars separately: Many Labs, RRR pair rows, PSA replication rows, Transparent Psi, ManyBabies 3, ManyClasses 2, ERN/Pe, self-control fMRI, Twomey, Linden, Protzko, AACT/ClinicalTrials.gov, CliniFact, Brodeur preregistered/PAP economics table tests, Nordic trial reporting, FReD, communication privacy, and retrieval-extinction rats.
- Most of those extra local sources are out because they are already Plot 1 replication-pair evidence, registry metadata rather than analytic preregistration, publication-linkage metadata without effect statistics, document/native-metric payloads without a compact D/N result table, or extracted table-test corpora without a focal/main-result selector.
- The considered list is still a working audit, not a claim that every preregistered-like source on the web has been exhausted.
